title: MCP Trust Registry — Trust Policy
description: Schema for a consumer-side trust policy file per protocol v0.1 §9. Stored locally (typically in YAML or JSON; this schema describes the canonical JSON form). Drives verifier decisions on attestation acceptance, capability grant, and revocation handling.

weight.description: Higher = consulted earlier. Default 0 (consultation order matches array order).

registries.description: Registries consulted in array order (or weight-adjusted). Registries themselves are NOT trust roots (§7.5).

trusted_publishers.description: Publishers whose attestations are accepted. Each entry identifies via ed25519 key OR OIDC subject+issuer (or both).

require_keyless.description: When true, only keyless (Sigstore-anchored) attestations are accepted.

require_co_attestations.description: Number of additional independent attestations required beyond the publisher's own. 0 = single attestation suffices.

default_capability_decision.description: Maps capability prefixes (e.g. 'fs:read', 'shell:*', 'secret:*') to default decisions. Most specific prefix wins. 'grant_if_required' = auto-grant when declared as required; 'grant_explicit' = always prompt; 'deny' = refuse.

on_unsigned_server.description: Behaviour when the server lacks any valid attestation. 'quarantine' = run with empty capability set (§9.1).

revocation_check_interval.description: Polling interval for revocation refresh. Go-style duration string.

audit_log_dir.description: Local filesystem path for the verifier's audit log (§11.2). Default is platform-specific.

template.description: Marks which reference template (§9.2) this policy derives from. Informational; verifier MUST NOT change behaviour based on this field.
